heading: Tenure and salaries of judges
label: 172 Tenure and salaries of judges
notes: Historical and Revision NotesThis section applies to the judges of the Court of Claims the same rule of precedence applicable to judges of other courts. (See sections 45, 136, 212, and 253 of this title.)

Amendments1992—Subsec. (a). substituted “United States Court of Federal Claims” for “United States Claims Court”.Pub. L. 102–5721988—Subsec. (b). amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (b) read as follows: “Each judge shall receive a salary at an annual rate determined under section 225 of the Federal Salary Act of 1967 (), as adjusted by .”Pub. L. 100–7022 U.S.C. 351–361section 461 of this title1982— amended section generally, substituting provisions relating to tenure and salaries of judges (formerly contained in section 173) for provisions relating to precedence of judges. See .Pub. L. 97–164section 171 of this title
Effective Date of 1992 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 102–572section 911 of Pub. L. 102–572section 171 of this titleOct. 29, 1992
Effective Date of 1982 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 97–164section 402 of Pub. L. 97–164section 171 of this titleOct. 1, 1982

children.label: a
children.text: Each judge of the United States Court of Federal Claims shall be appointed for a term of fifteen years.

children.label: b
children.text: Each judge shall receive a salary at the rate of pay, and in the same manner, as judges of the district courts of the United States.
